zh.Higham Gobion, Bedfordshire, England: 英國貝德福爾希加姆 格比
zh.Horton, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾霍頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Gobion III於大約1245年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾時，他的父親Sir Hugh Gobion III28歲，母親Matilda28歲。他於大約1282年與Margery Gobion結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1301年1月3日之前在英國。

zh-hans.Higham Gobion, Bedfordshire, England: 英国貝德福爾希加姆 格比
zh-hans.Horton, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾霍頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Gobion III于大约1245年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾时，他的父亲Sir Hugh Gobion III28岁，母亲Matilda28岁。他于大约1282年与Margery Gobion结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1301年1月3日之前在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Gobion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Bhola, Boodram, Mohan, Narine, Parmanand, Pooran, Shiva.  French Andre, Marcelle, Pierre.
French: from a pet form of   Gobert  .

variants.name: Gobert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Antoine, Marie-France, Monique.

French, German, and English (London): from a medieval personal name, Godebert, composed of the ancient Germanic elements gōd ‘good’ or god, got ‘god’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was popularized in England by the Normans, and probably absorbed an Old English form Godbeorht.

variants.name: Goben
meanings: Altered form of Gobin , a surname of English or French origin. In some cases however, the surname Goben (possibly of German origin; see 2 below) was changed to Gobin.
German (Göben): habitational name from Gobbin on Rügen Island (Pomerania) or perhaps from any of several places called Goben in Bavaria.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Gobion III was born about 1245, in Northamptonshire, England, his father, Sir Hugh Gobion III, was 28 and his mother, Matilda, was 28. He married Margery Gobion about 1282. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died before 3 January 1301, in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Gobion III est né environ 1245, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Hugh Gobion III, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Matilda, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Margery Gobion environ 1282. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 3 janvier 1301, à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Gobion III um 1245 in Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Hugh Gobion III 28 und seine Mutter Matilda 28. Er hat um 1282 Margery Gobion geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 3. Januar 1301 in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Gobion III è nato il circa 1245 nel Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Hugh Gobion III, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Matilda, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Margery Gobion circa 1282. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì prima del 3 gennaio 1301, nel England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Gobion IIIが約1245年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh Gobion IIIは28歳，母Matildaは28歳であった。約1282年にMargery Gobionと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1301年1月3日以前にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Gobion III(이)가 대략 1245년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Hugh Gobion III(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1282년 Margery Gobion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1301년 1월 3일 이전 영국에서 사망했다.

mn.about 1245–before 3 January 1301: ойролцоогоор 1245 он–1301 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 3–наас өмнө

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Gobion III nasceu aproximadamente 1245, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Hugh Gobion III, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Matilda, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Gobion aproximadamente 1282. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 3 de janeiro de 1301, em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Gobion III родился около 1245 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Hugh Gobion III было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Gobion около 1282 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер перед 3 январь 1301 г. в местности Англия.

sm.about 1245–before 3 January 1301: e tusa o le 1245–a'e le'I oo le 3 Ianuari 1301

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Gobion III nació aproximadamente 1245, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Hugh Gobion III, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Matilda, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Margery Gobion aproximadamente 1282. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió antes de 3 de enero de 1301, en Inglaterra.
